title: What Omnisciense is for humans & why we need to make that our goal

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that humans can achieve a form of 'omniscience' — not divine all-knowing, but a sufficient mastery of historical knowledge where understanding becomes greater than the sum of its parts. He defines this practically as being able to identify roughly 150 events per country (3 categories × 5 decades per century) and contends that universal historical literacy would eliminate political manipulation, bad-faith debates, and societal delusion. He connects this to a broader call for humanity to treat deep historical knowledge as a natural right and civilizational goal.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Achievable by ordinary people as mastery of historical pattern recognition, not divine knowledge
Without it, people are easily misled and argue from no shared basis
Example of someone with no knowledge foundation who spreads historical ignorance
Was caused by 90% top marginal tax rates, not cultural homogeneity as MAGA claims
Deflects historical evidence by falsely labeling it socialism
Children's time on games could be redirected to history study, which can be equally engaging
Collective omniscience is the only path to survival; ignorance leads to totalitarianism or destruction
Reflect lack of historical foundation — people impose dark-ages politics onto future tech instead of imagining real progress
